title: Herbal Stomach Growling Remedies: 9 Best Creams for Quick Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for stomach growling because it helps to reduce nausea and inflammation in the stomach, making it a natural remedy for soothing digestive issues.
Shogaol is good for stomach growling because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort that often causes growling.

Zingiberene is good for stomach growling because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the digestive tract, which can help ease stomach discomfort and reduce the rumbling noise associated with it.
Gingerols, also known as Lemon Grass, are good for stomach growling because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the digestive system and ease stomach discomfort.

Shogaols are good for stomach growling because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort that can cause stomach growling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is a natural remedy for stomach growling. This is because ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system. When you eat spicy or fatty foods, it can irritate the stomach lining, leading to discomfort and growling. Ginger cream can calm this irritation, reducing inflammation and promoting digestion.  Ginger also contains a compound called gingerol, which has been shown to have antispasmodic effects. This means it can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing cramps and spasms that can cause stomach growling. Additionally, ginger has natural antibacterial properties, which can help reduce bloating and discomfort caused by food poisoning or other digestive issues. Applying ginger cream to the stomach area can also provide quick relief from stomach growling. The cream is easily absorbed into the skin, allowing the active ingredients to reach the stomach directly. This can provide fast and effective relief from discomfort and pain, making it a popular natural remedy for stomach issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams that help with stomach growling, you'll need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you'll need to gather a few lemongrass stalks, also known as Cymbopogon citratus, and dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for a few days. Once the lemongrass is dry, you can grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, you'll need to mix the lemongrass powder with a carrier oil such as coconut oil or sweet almond oil in a ratio of about 1 part lemongrass powder to 3 parts carrier oil. You can also add some beeswax to thicken the mixture and give it a firmer texture. To do this, melt the carrier oil and beeswax together in a double boiler, then stir in the lemongrass powder and let the mixture cool and thicken. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, you can pour it into a container and let it set. The resulting cream can be applied topically to the stomach area to help soothe and calm digestive issues, including stomach growling.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for stomach growling

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can help with stomach growling due to its soothing properties. The active ingredient licorice root is a natural anti-inflammatory that calms the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. When we eat, our stomach produces digestive juices to break down food. Sometimes, these juices can be too strong and cause stomach growling. Glycyrrhiza glabra cream helps to balance the digestive juices, reducing the acidity and allowing the stomach to work efficiently. This natural remedy can also help to relax the stomach muscles, which can become tense and cause stomach growling. By using Glycyrrhiza glabra cream, you can soothe your stomach and get relief from growling.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal cream that helps with stomach growling, you will need to gather the necessary ingredients and equipment. Start by harvesting the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant, also known as lemongrass, which contains a natural compound that helps to ease digestion and reduce stomach discomfort. Dry the leaves completely in a warm, well-ventilated area, or by using a food dehydrator. Once the leaves are dry, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder. Next, combine the powdered lemongrass with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Heat the mixture gently, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the herbal properties of the lemongrass. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool completely. Finally, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discard the solids and store the herbal cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two weeks. When needed, apply a small amount of the cream to the stomach area to help soothe and calm stomach growling.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for stomach growling

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a popular herb used to help with stomach issues. The cream form of peppermint is easily absorbed by the body and can provide quick relief from stomach discomfort. When you apply peppermint cream to your stomach, the menthol in it helps to relax the muscles in your digestive system. This relaxation reduces the chances of stomach contractions, which can cause growling and discomfort. Peppermint also has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. As a result, the stomach growling and discomfort often associated with stomach contractions are reduced, providing a sense of relief. The menthol in peppermint cream also helps to stimulate digestion and improve the movement of food through the digestive system, further reducing stomach growling.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal cream that helps with stomach growling, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need fresh or dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and a carrier oil like sweet almond oil or jojoba oil. Begin by making a herbal infusion by steeping 1/4 cup of fresh or dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves in 2 cups of water for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the solids. Combine the herbal infusion with 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Melt the coconut oil and carrier oil, then add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and stir until it is fully incorporated. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency that is thick but still pourable. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely. Once the cream is set, you can apply it to the stomach area to help soothe stomach growling and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for stomach growling

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams, also known as fennel cream, is a popular remedy for stomach growling. The main reason it's effective is that it contains a compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply the cream to your stomach, the anethole helps to soothe any irritation or inflammation that may be causing the growling. This can be especially helpful if you've eaten something spicy or rich that's upset your stomach. Additionally, fennel has long been used as a natural digestive aid, helping to ease digestive issues like bloating and gas. By applying the cream directly to the stomach area, the anethole and other compounds in fennel can work quickly to calm the digestive system and reduce stomach growling. Some people also find that fennel cream helps to ease nausea and indigestion, making it a great natural remedy for stomach problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal cream for stomach growling, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you'll need to get some dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves, also known as lemongrass. You can buy these at a local health store or online. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of dried lemongrass leaves with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. After that, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, and let it cool down. Once it's cooled, you can add 1/4 cup of coconut oil or olive oil to the mixture. Then, heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals until the oil is melted and the mixture is well combined. Next, add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the mixture, and heat it again until the beeswax and shea butter are melted. Finally, pour the mixture into a small tin or jar, and let it cool and solidify before using it. This herbal cream can be applied to your stomach area to help soothe stomach growling and other digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for stomach growling

You can either grow them in your garden or buy them from a local health food store. Next, you will need to make a lemongrass tea by steeping the leaves in hot water for a few minutes, then let it cool down. After that, mix the cooled tea with a base oil such as coconut or olive oil in a 1:1 ratio, and add some beeswax to thicken the mixture. remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa cream, also known as turmeric cream, is a natural remedy that can help with stomach growling. The active compound in turmeric, called curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe digestive issues. When you apply the cream to your stomach, the curcumin works its way into your skin and is absorbed into your bloodstream. This can help to calm down inflammation in the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of stomach growling, such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The cream can also help to reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of stomach growling. By applying the cream to the stomach area, you can potentially reduce the frequency and severity of stomach growling episodes. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for people who experience stomach growling due to digestive issues, food intolerances, or other health conditions.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal cream that helps with stomach growling, you'll need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by infusing the Cymbopogon citratus leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. To do this, combine 1/2 cup of Cymbopogon citratus leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Next, mix 1/4 cup of shea butter or cocoa butter with 2 tablespoons of the infused oil and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until it's smooth and creamy, then remove it from the heat and let it cool until it solidifies. Once set, transfer the cream to a clean jar and store it in the fridge. When you need relief from stomach growling, apply a small amount of the Cymbopogon citratus cream to your abdomen and massage it in gently. The citronella oil in the Cymbopogon citratus leaves may help to calm your stomach and reduce hunger pangs.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal cream for stomach growling, you will need lemongrass essential oil, coconut oil, shea butter, and a carrier oil such as olive or sweet almond oil. First, combine 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a small saucepan and heat it on low heat until the shea butter is fully melted. Next, remove the saucepan from the heat and add 5-7 drops of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil, which has natural digestive properties that can help alleviate stomach growling. Stir the mixture well to combine. Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature, then mix in 1-2 tablespoons of a carrier oil such as olive or sweet almond oil. Transfer the mixture to a small glass jar and store it in a cool place. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to your stomach area as needed to help soothe and calm your digestive system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams, also known as black pepper cream, can be good for stomach growling. This is because piperine, a compound found in black pepper, has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When you eat a meal and your stomach starts to growl, it can be a sign of irritation. The piperine in the cream can help to calm down this irritation, reducing the discomfort and pain. The cream can also help to improve digestion, making it easier for your body to break down food. This can be especially helpful if you have a sensitive stomach or if you're experiencing digestive issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal cream that helps with stomach growling, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler. Begin by infusing the dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves in the coconut oil for about 2 weeks, shaking the mixture every few days to help the goodness from the leaves get into the oil. After the infusion, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the leaves. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler, then slowly add the coconut oil mixture, stirring constantly to prevent the mixture from separating. Finally, add a few drops of vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify, and your Cymbopogon citratus herbal cream is ready to use. To apply, simply massage a small amount of the cream onto your stomach before meals to help soothe and calm stomach growling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The oil extracted from its leaves contains a compound called citral, which is known to have anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically in a cream form, it can help ease digestive issues. The citral in lemongrass oil may help reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion and stomach cramps. This can help stop stomach growling by calming down the digestive system and reducing the discomfort associated with it. The cream's soothing properties may also help relax the muscles in the stomach, further reducing the sensation of hunger pangs. Additionally, lemongrass oil has natural antimicrobial properties, which can help combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to stomach discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams that help with stomach growling, you will need a few natural ingredients and some basic equipment. First, harvest or purchase some fresh lemongrass leaves (Cymbopogon citratus) and dry them completely to prevent spoilage. Next, mix 1 tablespoon of dried lemongrass with 1 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Leave the mixture to infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day, to allow the active compounds to transfer into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar, discarding the solids. Finally, mix 2-3 tablespoons of the lemongrass-infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring constantly until the mixture is smooth and creamy. Remove from heat, let cool, and transfer the herbal cream to a clean jar for use as needed to soothe stomach growling and indigestion.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams for stomach growling

intro_desc: Herbal creams can help alleviate stomach growling by incorporating soothing herbs like Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, and Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can calm digestive issues, while licorice root has anti-spasmodic effects that can ease stomach cramps and discomfort. When you experience stomach growling, it's essential to address the issue because it can be a sign of underlying digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or acid reflux. If left untreated, these issues can lead to chronic discomfort, nausea, and even malnutrition. Fixing stomach growling can significantly improve your life by allowing you to enjoy your favorite foods without discomfort, attend social events without anxiety, and maintain a healthy weight. By incorporating herbal creams containing ginger and licorice root into your daily routine, you can experience relief from stomach growling and take the first step towards a more comfortable and balanced life.
supplementary_best: To remedy stomach growling, a combination of Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, and Cuminum cyminum herbal creams is effective. Zingiber officinale contains gingerol, which helps to reduce nausea and inflammation in the stomach. Foeniculum vulgare contains fenchone, which has a soothing effect on the digestive system and helps to ease stomach cramps. Cuminum cyminum contains cuminaldehyde, which helps to stimulate digestion and relieve bloating. The combination of these three herbs provides a synergistic effect, where the anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale complement the soothing effects of Foeniculum vulgare, while Cuminum cyminum enhances digestion. This combination helps to calm stomach growling, reduce nausea, and promote a feeling of fullness, making it an effective remedy for stomach discomfort.
supplementary_avoid: If you have stomach growling, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One such herb is 'Valeriana officinalis', which is known to slow down digestion and worsen stomach issues. It can cause a feeling of nausea and make stomach growling worse. Another herb to avoid is 'Aconitum napellus', which is highly toxic and can cause stomach problems, including nausea and vomiting, when applied topically. Similarly, 'Aloe vera' can cause stomach issues if ingested in large amounts, and applying its cream may cause stomach growling to worsen due to its potential to slow down digestion. 'Artemisia absinthium' and 'Hydrastis canadensis' are also best avoided, as they can cause stomach upset and worsen stomach growling due to their bitter and stimulating properties.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger is a natural remedy that can help with stomach growling. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and discomfort in the stomach. You can make ginger tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water or take ginger in the form of capsules or powder.  Peppermint oil, when inhaled, can also help ease stomach discomfort and reduce growling. This is because peppermint oil has a calming effect on the digestive system. You can add a few drops of peppermint oil to a warm bath or inhale it through a diffuser.  Aloe vera juice is another remedy that can help soothe an irritated stomach and reduce growling. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation. Drinking aloe vera juice after meals can help ease stomach discomfort and reduce growling.  Probiotics are beneficial bacteria that help maintain a healthy balance of gut bacteria. When the gut bacteria are out of balance, it can cause stomach growling and discomfort. Taking probiotics in the form of supplements or eating probiotic-rich foods like yogurt can help restore the balance of gut bacteria and reduce stomach growling.  Fennel seeds have been used for centuries to help ease stomach discomfort and reduce growling. Fennel seeds have carminative properties that help release trapped gas in the stomach and reduce bloating. You can chew on fennel seeds after meals or drink fennel tea to help ease stomach discomfort.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience stomach growling may also have other ailments like bloating, gas, and indigestion. This is because stomach growling is often a sign of poor digestion. One common issue is low stomach acid, which can be caused by stress, age, or a diet lacking essential nutrients. Medicinal herbs like ginger and peppermint can help alleviate these symptoms. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. Peppermint oil can also ease digestion and relieve bloating by relaxing the muscles in the stomach. Another herb that may help is licorice root, which can help heal and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. These herbs work by either increasing stomach acid production, relaxing stomach muscles, or reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
